issues.title: Bloat: remove stale MASTER_CONTEXT_PACKET mirror and keep docs single-source
issues.description:
## Why
`MASTER_CONTEXT_PACKET.md` currently mirrors almost all packet docs (`docs/01..22`) and also contains stale snapshots (for example old `STATUS.md` state). This duplicates context and creates conflicting instructions for AI agents.

## Evidence
- `MASTER_CONTEXT_PACKET.md` is 2,755 lines / 74,208 bytes.
- Line-level containment from packet docs into master packet is 100% for most docs and >=88% for all `docs/01..22`.
- Stale phrases exist only in master packet (for example "Product implementation not started yet").

## Scope
1. Make `docs/*.md` + `README.md` the only canonical packet sources.
2. Replace `MASTER_CONTEXT_PACKET.md` with a generated lightweight index (or remove it and update references).
3. Add a guard test that fails if packet docs are re-embedded verbatim into a second file.
4. Update `AGENTS.md`/`README.md` references to the canonical source.

## Out of Scope
- Product behavior changes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No duplicated full packet mirror remains in tracked files.
- [ ] No stale instructions remain discoverable in packet-level docs.
- [ ] Agent entry docs clearly identify one canonical packet path.

## Verification
- `npm test`
- `rg -n "Product implementation not started yet" README.md docs MASTER_CONTEXT_PACKET.md`
- `git ls-files '*.md' | wc -l` and confirm only intended packet docs remain.

issues.title: Bloat: trim STATUS/PLANS to active window and archive historical detail
issues.description:
## Why
`STATUS.md` and `PLANS.md` carry large historical sections that increase context consumption for every agent run. Historical data belongs in archive files, not hot-path context files.

## Evidence
- `STATUS.md`: 561 lines / 33,508 bytes.
- `PLANS.md`: 616 lines / 25,251 bytes.
- Root markdown (`README.md`, `STATUS.md`, `PLANS.md`, `PLANS_ARCHIVE.md`, `MASTER_CONTEXT_PACKET.md`) is 6,089 lines.

## Scope
1. Define strict size budgets for hot-path docs (for example `STATUS.md <= 320 lines`, `PLANS.md <= 350 lines`).
2. Move detailed historical verification logs from `STATUS.md` into structured archive files under `evidence/` or a dedicated `status-archive/`.
3. Keep only current objective, current environments, last accepted milestone, and latest evidence pointers in `STATUS.md`.
4. Add automated policy test(s) enforcing line-count and section-count budgets.

## Out of Scope
- Deleting historical evidence artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `STATUS.md` and `PLANS.md` are within defined hot-path budgets.
- [ ] Historical detail remains accessible via linked archives.
- [ ] Policy tests fail when budget is exceeded.

## Verification
- `npm test`
- `wc -l STATUS.md PLANS.md`
- `npm run plans:compact`

issues.title: Bloat: enforce evidence retention and exclude oversized raw logs from git
issues.description:
## Why
Tracked evidence payloads currently dominate repository bytes and are not bounded by a retention policy. This inflates clone size and context search noise.

## Evidence
- Tracked repository size: 2,307,622 bytes.
- `evidence/render-log-audit-all.ndjson` alone is 1,319,070 bytes (57.16% of tracked bytes).
- `evidence/` contributes ~1.47 MiB of tracked data across 74 files.

## Scope
1. Define evidence classes: `summary` (tracked), `raw` (ignored or compressed external artifact), `ephemeral`.
2. Add `.gitignore` rules for raw oversized evidence (for example `evidence/**/*.ndjson`, `evidence/**/*.raw`) while preserving curated summaries.
3. Add script to generate compact summaries from raw logs and optionally gzip raw outputs.
4. Refactor verification scripts to write summaries by default and raw logs only behind an explicit debug flag.
5. Add a repository size budget check for evidence files (for example no single tracked evidence file > 200 KB).

## Out of Scope
- Rewriting unrelated business logic.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No oversized raw log artifacts are tracked by default.
- [ ] Verification still produces actionable evidence summaries.
- [ ] Repo hygiene check fails when evidence budget is exceeded.

## Verification
- `npm test`
- `find evidence -type f -maxdepth 1 -exec du -k {} + | sort -nr | head`
- `git ls-files -z | xargs -0 du -k | sort -nr | head`

issues.title: Bloat: extract shared UI primitives for booking and walk-in pages
issues.description:
## Why
The booking and walk-in UI renderers duplicate most of their logic and inline styling, increasing maintenance cost and agent token usage.

## Evidence
- `src/ui/appointmentBookingPage.js` and `src/ui/walkInIntakePage.js` have ~90% normalized line containment overlap.
- File sizes: 618 and 578 lines respectively.

## Scope
1. Extract shared helpers/components (escaping, field-error rendering, lookup tables, shared CSS tokens/layout, preserved field handling) into `src/ui/pageFormShared.js` (or equivalent).
2. Keep page-specific copy and fields in small adapter modules.
3. Preserve Russian UI text and current HTML contracts used by tests/scenarios.
4. Add focused tests for shared renderer primitives if needed.

## Out of Scope
- Redesigning UI copy or business flow.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Cross-file duplication between the two UI modules is materially reduced (target <35% containment).
- [ ] Existing booking/walk-in page tests remain green with unchanged behavior.
- [ ] Shared module boundaries are explicit and documented.

## Verification
- `npm test`
- `npm run verify`
- `npx jscpd --min-lines 6 --min-tokens 50 --format javascript src/ui`

issues.title: Bloat: unify booking and walk-in page route orchestration
issues.description:
## Why
`appointmentPageRoutes` and `walkInIntakePageRoutes` reimplement the same orchestration patterns (form normalization, lookup resolution, inline entity creation, localized validation mapping, error rendering).

## Evidence
- `src/http/appointmentPageRoutes.js` vs `src/http/walkInIntakePageRoutes.js` has ~71% normalized line containment overlap.
- Pair contributes heavily to jscpd duplication hotspots.

## Scope
1. Introduce shared page-flow orchestration module(s) in `src/http/` for common concerns.
2. Keep route-specific behavior (appointment capacity conflict vs walk-in flow specifics) in thin adapters.
3. Preserve endpoint contracts and current localized error strings.
4. Reduce duplicated branching and helper function copies.

## Out of Scope
- API contract changes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Shared orchestration extracted with clear interfaces.
- [ ] Route files are significantly smaller and easier to diff.
- [ ] Booking/walk-in integration tests and verify scenarios continue to pass.

## Verification
- `npm test`
- `npm run verify`
- `npx jscpd --min-lines 6 --min-tokens 50 --format javascript src/http`

issues.title: Bloat: consolidate page and scheduling scenario scripts into shared engine
issues.description:
## Why
Scenario runners duplicate mode resolution, request wrappers, form submission, and assertion scaffolding across three large scripts.

## Evidence
- `scripts/booking-page-scenario.js`, `scripts/walkin-page-scenario.js`, and `scripts/scheduling-walkin-scenario.js` are 462/341/442 lines.
- jscpd reports repeated clone groups across these files.

## Scope
1. Build a shared scenario runtime (`scripts/scenario-core.js`) for mode detection, diagnostics, HTTP helpers, and output formatting.
2. Convert each scenario file into a thin composition layer that declares steps and assertions.
3. Keep existing CLI commands and output JSON shape backward compatible.
4. Update tests to cover shared runtime behavior.

## Out of Scope
- Changing scenario business expectations.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Common scenario code exists in one reusable module.
- [ ] Legacy npm commands still work.
- [ ] Duplication across scenario scripts materially reduced.

## Verification
- `npm test`
- `npm run verify`
- `node scripts/booking-page-scenario.js --non-destructive`
- `node scripts/walkin-page-scenario.js --non-destructive`
- `node scripts/scheduling-walkin-scenario.js --non-destructive`

issues.title: Bloat: de-duplicate booking and walk-in page integration tests
issues.description:
## Why
Booking and walk-in page tests duplicate setup and assertion structure, increasing maintenance cost and review noise without increasing coverage proportionally.

## Evidence
- `tests/appointmentBookingPage.test.js` and `tests/walkInIntakePage.test.js` show ~52% normalized line containment overlap.
- Both files repeat near-identical server lifecycle and form-submit scaffolding.

## Scope
1. Extract shared page-test harness utilities (server setup, form submit helpers, redirect extraction, common assertions).
2. Introduce data-driven test matrix where possible to avoid copy-paste tests.
3. Keep route-specific behaviors as explicit separate cases.
4. Preserve or improve existing coverage.

## Out of Scope
- Removing critical behavior assertions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Significant duplication removed between booking/walk-in page tests.
- [ ] All existing page-flow behavior remains validated.
- [ ] Test files are shorter and easier to extend.

## Verification
- `npm test`
- `npm run verify`
- `npx jscpd --min-lines 6 --min-tokens 50 --format javascript tests`

issues.title: Bloat: add automated bloat and duplication budgets to CI
issues.description:
## Why
Current duplication and context growth were allowed because no automated budgets exist. Without gates, bloat will regress with agent-generated code/docs/tests.

## Scope
1. Add `npm run audit:bloat` script to compute:
   - tracked bytes by area (`src`, `tests`, `scripts`, `docs`, `evidence`),
   - top tracked file sizes,
   - jscpd duplication %,
   - hot-path doc line counts (`README`, `STATUS`, `PLANS`).
2. Add machine-readable report output under `evidence/` (or temporary CI artifact).
3. Define initial thresholds with ratchet policy (cannot get worse without explicit override).
4. Wire gate into CI workflow and local verify docs.

## Out of Scope
- Immediate refactor of all existing duplication; this task adds enforcement and baseline.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CI fails when duplication/size budgets regress beyond threshold.
- [ ] Budget report is reproducible locally.
- [ ] Thresholds and override protocol are documented.

## Verification
- `npm run audit:bloat`
- `npm test`
- CI workflow run showing pass/fail behavior on threshold breach.
